name: Coll de la Llose and Lake Matemale Loop
name_original: Entre le col de Juell et de la Llose
description: This difficult 20.5-mile (32.9 km) racebike route between Juell Pass and Llose Pass climbs 3161 feet (963 meters) in the French Pyrenees.
region_intro: Hiking around Railleu offers access to a mountainous and heavily wooded environment within the Pyrénées-Orientales region of Occitanie, France. The area is part of the Garrotxes, a section of the Parc Naturel Régional des Pyrénées Catalanes, providing preserved natural settings. Hikers can expect varied landscapes, including valleys, forests, and views of the Ribera de Cabrils. The terrain alternates between open, exposed areas and sheltered passages through dense undergrowth. Best hiking trails around Railleu The most popular hiking route is Puig de l'Orri – Puig dels Agrellons loop from Sansa, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route offers varied landscapes and passes through the neighboring village of Sansa. Another top favourite among local hikers is Col de la Creu – Puig dels Agrellons loop from Col de Creu, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This trail features diverse scenery with an elevation gain of 1020 feet (311 meters). Local hikers also love the Lake Caudiès – Col de Juell 1,500 m loop from Railleu, a 9.9 miles (15.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Railleu is defined by mountainous and wooded terrain, diverse valleys, and preserved natural environments. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Railleu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 2100 reviews. More than 16500 hikers have used komoot to explore Railleu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Railleu?
A: There are over 280 hiking trails around Railleu, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels. This includes approximately 110 easy routes, 130 moderate routes, and 45 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for everyone to explore in the mountainous and wooded terrain of the Pyrénées-Orientales.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking near Railleu?
A: The terrain around Railleu is predominantly mountainous and heavily wooded, situated within the Pyrénées Catalanes Regional Natural Park. You'll encounter diverse landscapes, from serene forests and green valleys to impressive natural features like canyons and mountain passes. Trails often alternate between open, exposed areas and sheltered passages through dense undergrowth, providing varied scenery and challenges.

Q: Are there any circular walks recommended around Railleu?
A: Yes, Railleu offers several excellent circular walks. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39999912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Creu – Puig dels Agrellons loop from Col de Creu&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 10.2 km. For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24681979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Caudiès – Col de Juell 1,500 m loop from Railleu&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 15.9 km trail through mountainous terrain.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to discover on a hike?
A: The region around Railleu is rich in natural beauty. You can explore dramatic canyons like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/260391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carança Corniche&lt;/a&gt;, known for its paths carved into the rock, or discover the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/873604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Footbridges of the Carançà Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. For stunning vistas, look for viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panorama from Pic Carlit to Cambre d'Aze&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/612655" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Coll del Pam (2005 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options near Railleu?
A: While many trails in Railleu involve significant elevation, there are options suitable for families. The region research mentions "Le Sentier des 5 sens" (The Path of the 5 Senses) accessible from the Col de Jau road, which offers a unique sensory experience in nature and is generally suitable for a wider audience. For specific easy routes, you can filter the available 110 easy trails on komoot to find shorter, less strenuous options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Railleu?
A: The Pyrénées Catalanes Regional Natural Park, where Railleu is located, generally welcomes dogs on trails, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. It's always best to check specific trail regulations or local signage, but many of the natural paths are suitable for dog walking, allowing you and your canine companion to enjoy the serene environment.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Railleu?
A: The hiking trails around Railleu are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.6 out of 5 stars from over 2,100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the preserved natural settings, the diverse landscapes ranging from forests to valleys, and the tranquility of the area away from mass tourism.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through charming villages or historical sites?
A: Yes, some trails offer a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest. The region research notes that routes like the "Boucle autour de Railleu" allow visitors to discover picturesque villages such as Railleu itself and the neighboring Sansa. You might also encounter historical elements like the vestiges of an ancient Romanesque church or an old mill near a stone bridge along certain paths.

Q: What is a good moderate hike to experience the local scenery?
A: For a moderate hike that showcases the local scenery, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20536107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Creu – Refuge du Coll de Torn loop from Col de Creu&lt;/a&gt;. This 7.5 km route offers diverse views and is a great way to immerse yourself in the mountainous and wooded environment of Railleu without being overly strenuous.

Q: Are there any lakes accessible via hiking trails near Railleu?
A: Yes, the area features several beautiful lakes. The region research mentions the Estanyols area with its various lakes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3517053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Aude&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable natural monument. For a challenging hike that includes a lake, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24681979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Caudiès – Col de Juell 1,500 m loop from Railleu&lt;/a&gt; is an option, though it's a difficult route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Railleu?
A: Railleu offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. The mountainous and wooded environment is beautiful in spring and autumn with vibrant foliage. Summer provides warmer weather for higher altitude trails, while winter can offer a unique experience with animal tracks in fresh snow, though some routes may require specific gear or be inaccessible due to snow. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.
tips: Small pass, at the bend of a bend, on a municipal road not necessarily very well cleaned, but there is no one, it's great!

It is a town to breathe the tranquility of the mountains in all its expression. It is located halfway between the climb to the Col de la Llosa and the Coll de Jouell, therefore, it is a common stopover for cyclists who cover the Route du Cols.
Like any good town, it has a beautiful fountain where we can recharge water for the challenges we are going to face.

The coll de la Llosa, with an altitude of 1866 meters, is a mountain pass that separates the plateau from the Quillane pass with the Capcir region, north of the Tossa peak.
On this side it is very simple and fast, but from it you can start countless loops.
Very large lake (retained on the Aude) with a beautiful view of the two roads that go down there. The one that goes to Angles seemed to me more pleasant in terms of curve, and circulation.

faqs.question: Where exactly is this cycling route located?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route is located in the French Pyrenees, specifically within the Parc Naturel Régional des Pyrénées Catalanes. It connects two notable mountain passes in the region: Col de Juell 1,500 m and Coll de la Llose from La Llagonne.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically from late spring (May) through early autumn (October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the passes are clear of snow, offering optimal conditions for road cycling in the Pyrenees.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of well-paved mountain roads, characteristic of Pyrenean passes. You can expect sustained climbs with an even gradient, particularly on the ascent to Coll de la Llose from La Llagonne. While mostly paved, some very short segments might be less maintained or have rougher surfaces, requiring good bike handling skills. The route is rated as 'difficult' due to its significant elevation gain over 32.9 km.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Parc Naturel Régional des Pyrénées Catalanes?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Parc Naturel Régional des Pyrénées Catalanes. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning natural beauty and panoramic views of the Pyrenean landscapes. You'll pass through the settlement of Ayguatébia and near Lake Matemale. The highlight, of course, is conquering the mountain passes themselves, Col de Juell 1,500 m and Coll de la Llose from La Llagonne, which provide expansive vistas.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation gain of 963.4 meters, and a distance of nearly 33 km, this route is generally not recommended for beginner road cyclists. It requires good to very good fitness and experience with mountain climbs. Beginners might find the sustained ascents and descents overly challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking areas for this exact route are not detailed, but you can typically find parking in the towns or villages near the start or end points of the passes, such as La Llagonne or Olette, which are common starting points for climbs in the region. Always check local signage for parking restrictions.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several local paths. You will find yourself on parts of El Camí IT1TR4 El Camí Capçaler: de Salzes a Balaguer, Chemin Vauban, L'Espinassera, Chemin de Mont-Louis à Ayguatébia, Chemin de Sansa, Chemin d'Ayguatébia à Olette, and Cami de la Costa. These intersections offer opportunities to explore further if you wish to extend your ride.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route on public roads, dogs are generally permitted. However, if you plan to stop or venture off-road into natural areas, especially within the Parc Naturel Régional des Pyrénées Catalanes, always keep your dog on a leash and adhere to any specific park regulations regarding pets to protect wildlife and ensure safety.

faqs.question: What should I wear for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mountain environment, weather can change rapidly. It's advisable to wear layers, including a breathable base layer, a cycling jersey, arm/leg warmers, and a lightweight windproof/waterproof jacket. Even on sunny days, descents can be cool. Don't forget a helmet, cycling gloves, and appropriate cycling shoes.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or get water along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the settlement of Ayguatébia, where you might find limited services. It's always best to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire 33 km route, especially since services can be sparse in mountain regions. Plan your stops in advance.

waypoints.name: Coll de la Llose from La Llagonne
